subject: What HR software should we use?

day_1.subject: What HR software should we use?
day_1.detail: works with businesses on HR and people support
day_1.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

When one of your clients asks, "what HR software should we use?", the easy answer is to recommend Breathe, BambooHR, Personio or another third-party platform.

That may solve the immediate problem, but it also means the software relationship sits with someone else.

Vesra is based in York, and we help HR consultancies offer clients a branded HR platform alongside their existing advice: your brand, your client relationship, no software build or maintenance required.

The short version is that you keep the software layer close to the consultancy, create a monthly recurring revenue line, and give clients a more modern HR experience between advice calls.

I recorded a short partner overview here:
https://vesra.io/white-label-partner-overview

Worth a 15-minute conversation?

Best,
Chris

follow_ups.subject: Re: What HR software should we use?

steps.subject: What HR software should we use?
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

steps.subject: Re: What HR software should we use?
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

Quick follow-up, but rather than just nudging you, this is the bit I think matters.

The HR consultancies that tend to see the strongest fit with Vesra are usually already being asked by clients what HR software they should use.

Most firms answer that by recommending a third-party platform. Useful for the client, but commercially it gives away the software opportunity.

Vesra gives you a way to offer the platform under your own brand, while keeping the advisory relationship at the centre.

Happy to send a one-pager if easier than a call.

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: 10 retained clients
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

One reason HR consultancies look at Vesra is that it changes the shape of the client relationship.

If 10 retained clients paid a monthly fee for a branded HR platform alongside your consultancy service, that starts to become a meaningful recurring revenue line.

The point is not to replace the advice. It is to give clients the software layer they increasingly expect while keeping your consultancy as the trusted partner.

No big software build. No product team. Just a different way to package the expertise and client relationships you already have.

Is it worth 15 minutes?

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: Closing this one out
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

I have reached out a few times and have not heard back, so I will leave this here unless it is relevant.

The reason I got in touch is that Vesra is built for HR consultancies that want to offer clients a branded HR platform without becoming a software company themselves.

If that is something you are likely to explore later, just reply "not yet" and I can check back another time.

If it is not relevant, no problem at all.

P.S. The overview is short if useful:
https://vesra.io/white-label-partner-overview

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: Revenue without additional consulting hours
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

Many HR consultancies are looking for ways to grow revenue without continually adding more consulting hours.

One approach we are seeing is consultancies offering a branded HR platform alongside their existing advisory services.

Rather than simply recommending third-party software, they provide the platform themselves and generate recurring monthly revenue from existing client relationships.

The goal is not to become a software company.

It is to create a more scalable service model while continuing to deliver the advice and support clients value.

I recorded a short overview here:
https://vesra.io/white-label-partner-overview

Is this something you have considered before?

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: Re: Revenue without additional consulting hours
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

A quick follow-up.

Most HR consultancies grow by winning more clients or increasing consulting hours.

The firms looking at Vesra are often exploring a third option: creating recurring software revenue from clients they already support.

It is a simple idea, but it can materially change the economics of a consultancy business.

Happy to send more information if useful.

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: A different way to grow
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

One thing we have noticed is that many consultancy owners want growth, but not necessarily more delivery work.

Offering a branded HR platform allows consultancies to increase revenue per client while continuing to focus on advice and support.

No software development.

No technical team.

Just another revenue stream built on relationships you already have.

Worth a conversation?

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: Close the loop?
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

I appreciate this may not be a priority right now.

The reason I reached out is that we are helping HR consultancies create recurring software revenue alongside their advisory services.

If that is not something you are exploring, no problem at all.

Just let me know and I will close the file.

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: Becoming harder to replace
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

Clients increasingly expect more than advice alone.

They want software, self-service and a central place to manage HR activities.

When that software relationship sits with another provider, they become embedded in the client organisation every day.

Vesra allows HR consultancies to offer clients a branded HR platform alongside their advisory services.

The result is stronger client retention and a deeper relationship with the organisations you support.

I recorded a short overview here:
https://vesra.io/white-label-partner-overview

Worth taking a look?

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: Re: Becoming harder to replace
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

A quick follow-up.

Many HR consultancies do fantastic advisory work, but the software layer often sits elsewhere.

The challenge is that clients interact with the software every day and the consultancy far less frequently.

That is one reason some firms are looking to offer both.

Happy to share more if useful.

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: More than advice
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

The consultancies that seem best positioned for the next few years are combining expertise with technology.

Advice remains incredibly valuable, but clients increasingly expect a platform alongside the service.

Vesra helps consultancies deliver both under their own brand.

No software team required.

Worth exploring?

Best,
Chris

steps.subject: Final follow-up
steps.body_template:
Hi {first_name},

I have reached out a few times because we are helping HR consultancies strengthen client retention through a branded HR platform.

If it is not relevant, no problem at all.

If it is something you would like to revisit later, simply reply "not yet" and I will leave it there for now.

Best,
Chris
